title: Why is ISIS  so crazy   Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request asking why ISIS is so violent and extreme. He argues that ISIS's behavior stems primarily from prolonged war, trauma, and stress rather than religion itself, and that religion is used as a unifying tool by people already pushed to a breaking point. He also traces the region's lack of education to historical events like the Mongol invasions and geographic isolation.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Prolonged war and trauma cause psychological breakdown into 'warrior mode,' not religion itself
Religion is used as a rallying/unifying tool by already-broken people, not the cause of their extremism
A grave mistake that destabilized a delicate balance and caused the current chaos
Lead by example with education and freedom, not military force or torture
Wars last about five years; ISIS will burn out on its own without Western intervention
Both Christian and Muslim fundamentalists are trying to provoke an apocalyptic battle to trigger their utopia
Rooted in geographic isolation and the Mongol destruction of the region's intellectual civilization, not inherent to the people
Christians were the bad guys; Muslims behaved more humanely
Strategic provocation targeting 'the outside world,' not a new phenomenon — just newly filmed
